channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: The fire hydrant post has fallen, 6th April
item.description:
What3words vets.marker.wiped. On the wall/pavement between the park and St George’s church. The concrete fire hydrant post has broken off. Part is on the ground.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38-40, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sofa and mattress dumped, 6th April
item.description:

item.title: Lesnes Abbey Easter Festival 2026 - dangerous parking, 6th April
item.description:
Please pass this report to the organisers of the Lesnes Abbey Easter Festival. Yet again, the roads around Lesnes Abbey Woods have had parking problems, courtesy of the Easter Festival. This year's parking can only be described as downright dangerous. The attached photos were taken at 16:03 on Saturday 4 April, and show four vehicles parked on the double-yellow lines next to the park entrance on Abbey Road. The close-up photo shows that two of the cars are parked right next to the pedestrian refuge island, leaving a very narrow gap. Small cars travelling towards Abbey Wood were just about able to get through this narrow gap, but larger vehicles were forced onto the wrong side of the road. There could easily have been an accident. Some of the 'parked' vehicles had a driver and hazard lights flashing (which is not an excuse for parking illegally), so they were presumably waiting to pick people up from the festival. Someone in one of the 'parked' cars gave a mouthful of abuse to someone who was trying to drive through the small gap. Surely it is time to move this event to a venue that has a car park?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post broken, 6th April
item.description:
